title: Force Push to Protected Branches
dataSources: Push events with `forced=true` and `ref` matching protected branch patterns
spl:
index=devops sourcetype="github:webhook" event="push" forced="true"
| where match(ref,"refs/heads/(main|master|release/.*|production)")
| table _time, repository, ref, pusher, forced
| sort -_time
description: Narrows UC-12.1.6 to default/release branches where history rewrite has highest impact.
value: Narrows UC-12.1.6 to default/release branches where history rewrite has highest impact.
implementation: Maintain lookup of protected ref regex per org. Page on-call for production branch force pushes. Exclude documented release bot service accounts.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: GitHub/GitLab webhooks.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: Push events with `forced=true` and `ref` matching protected branch patterns.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Maintain lookup of protected ref regex per org. Page on-call for production branch force pushes. Exclude documented release bot service accounts.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=devops sourcetype="github:webhook" event="push" forced="true"
| where match(ref,"refs/heads/(main|master|release/.*|production)")
| table _time, repository, ref, pusher, forced
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Force Push to Protected Branches** ‚Äî Narrows UC-12.1.6 to default/release branches where history rewrite has highest impact.

Documented **Data sources**: Push events with `forced=true` and `ref` matching protected branch patterns. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): GitHub/GitLab webhooks. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: devops; **sourcetype**: github:webhook. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=devops, sourcetype="github:webhook". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where match(ref,"refs/heads/(main|master|release/.*|production)")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **Force Push to Protected Branches**): table _time, repository, ref, pusher, forced
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (force pushes), Timeline, Bar chart (by branch).
visualization: Table (force pushes), Timeline, Bar chart (by branch).
